content.text: You have 2 hours to answer the following questions. You must show all your work as neatly and clearly as possible and indicate the final answer clearly. You may not use a calculator.

body.text:
The iteration
\[
x_{n+1}=x_n-\frac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)-\frac{1}{2}f''(x_n)\frac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)}}
\]
for solving the equation \(f(x)=0\) is known as Halley’s method.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the method can, alternatively, be interpreted as applying Newton’s method to the equation \(g(x)=0\), with \(g(x)=f(x)/\sqrt{f'(x)}\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Assuming \(\alpha\) is a simple root of the equation, and \(x_n\to\alpha\) as \(n\to\infty\), show that convergence is at least cubic. Hint: Part (a) might help.

body.text: Do the following parts which are unrelated.

subparts.label: (a)
(b)

body.text:
Consider a quadrature rule of the form
\[
\int_0^1 f(x)\,dx\approx Af(0)+Bf'(0)+Cf(\gamma)+Df(1).
\]

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Determine the constants \(A,B,C,D\), and \(\gamma\) so that the quadrature formula has maximum degree of exactness.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: What is the degree of exactness of the formula in part (a)?

body.text: For the function \(f(x)=\ln(x)\) for \(x\in[1,2]\), find the minimax approximation polynomial of degree one. Give the exact value of the minimax error.
This problem has the following parts.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Relative to the \(L^2\) norm on the interval \([1,3]\), find the least squares approximation to the function \(f(x)=1/x\) using polynomials of degree at most one.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Find the least squares error of the approximation above.
